name: Jimmy Davis
description: American songwriter, singer, pianist and actor
abstract: James Edward Davis (1915 – 1997) was an American songwriter, composer, singer, pianist and actor. He co-wrote the song "Lover Man (Oh, Where Can You Be?)".

about_quotes.speaker: Emmanuel Sayles
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Jimmy Davis, he had a job for me at, uh, show bar... In round about, uh, 60, I went back with this fella, Jimmy Davis. Mm-hmm. We had a trio, uh, Andrew Johnson on drums, Jimmy Davis on piano, and, uh, me on electric guitar

about_quotes.speaker: Chester Jones
about_quotes.evidence: We were on bass Jimmy Davis on bass We were there for seven weeks

about_quotes.speaker: Bennie Turner
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee confirms the identity of 'Jimmy Davis' only to clarify that the person being discussed is Jimmy Davis' brother, not Jimmy Davis himself. No direct interaction or personal relationship with Jimmy Davis is described.

about_quotes.speaker: Lionel Ferbos
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions 'Jimmy Davis, the pianist' and describes events involving him, but the interviewee (Lionel Ferbos) does not respond with any personal anecdote, recognition, or statement indicating a direct relationship.

about_quotes.speaker: George Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: Robert, the boy named Jim, Jimmy Davis' brother, Robert Davis, he was a drummer. Was the governor? Was Jimmy Davis the governor? ... No, Jimmy Davis the check and click. Jim, Jim, Jim did.

about_quotes.speaker: Joseph "Fan" Bourgeau
about_quotes.evidence: The governor. Our governor wrote that. Big Buck Blues. Which governor was this? Not so long. He was trying to get back to him. Jimmy Davis. Jimmy Davis.
